mnemonics.title: Mnemonic 1
mnemonics.content: Good Fit Chi-Square: Categories Have Important Squared Gaps, Fitting Is The Goal

mnemonics.title: Key Point 1
mnemonics.content: The test compares observed frequencies in categories to expected frequencies under a null hypothesis model.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 2
mnemonics.content: The test statistic is calculated as the sum of (Observed - Expected)^2 / Expected for each category.

mnemonics.title: Key Point 3
mnemonics.content: A large chi-square statistic (relative to the chi-square distribution with appropriate degrees of freedom) leads to rejection of the null hypothesis, suggesting a poor fit.
